assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_015",
  "rule_id": "Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Digit Characters",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "rationale": "The remediation only updates the PAM pwquality configuration (dcredit) to enforce digit usage in passwords. This is a standard STIG password policy change, does not affect system boot or critical services, and can be applied without risk of immediate lockout if performed correctly.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Incorrect value could tighten policy beyond user expectations",
    "Potential temporary user inconvenience until passwords are changed"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Backup existing /etc/security/pwquality.conf and any *.conf files in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/",
    "Set dcredit=-1 (or desired negative value) in the appropriate configuration file",
    "Run `pwquality-check` or test password changes for compliance",
    "Document the change and inform users of updated password requirements"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": true,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
assistant_message.reasoning:
We need to classify the finding: "Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Digit Characters". The remediation changes dcredit setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf (or pwquality.conf.d). This is a PAM password policy change. Usually safe to remediate; no reboot needed, touches authentication and authorization (pam). Not network or filesystem. So category safe_to_remediate. Confidence high, maybe 0.97.

Provide rationale: modifying dcredit is standard PAM config, low risk. risk_factors: possible lockout if mishandled; need to ensure value correct; but safe.

safe_next_steps: edit /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf or /etc/security/pwquality.conf to set dcredit=-1 (require at least one digit), verify password changes, test with new password, ensure no lockout.

requires_reboot: false.

touches_authn_authz: true.

touches_networking: false.

touches_filesystems: false.

Now create JSON.
